foyer.title: Engine Foyer
foyer.description:
You are standing in the foyer of the Text Adventure Engine demonstration. Doorways lead off in several directions, each demonstrating a different engine feature. A welcome plaque is mounted on the wall.

Type commands like `go north` or `examine plaque`, or click the labelled exits and items below.

items.label: welcome plaque
items.description: A polished brass plaque reads: 'Welcome to the Text Adventure Engine. This world is self-referential — each room demonstrates a feature. Once you have explored all four wings, the door to the conclusion room will open.'

exits-room.title: Exits Room
exits-room.description: This room demonstrates exits. There are three doorways here. The first is open. The second is locked (you can see it, but you can't go through it — the wrapper or standalone mode marks it inaccessible). The third leads back to the foyer.

items-room.title: Items Room
items-room.description: Three items sit on a table. The brass coin can be examined freely. The crystal shard is locked — visible but you can't interact with it. The third item demonstrates collection: pick up the wooden token and it becomes greyed out.

items.label: brass coin
items.description: A small brass coin, dull from age.

items.label: crystal shard (locked)
items.description: A shimmering crystal shard. (You can't actually examine this — it's marked inaccessible.)

items.label: wooden token
items.description: A small wooden token. Examining it 'collects' it (in standalone mode).

inventory-room.title: Inventory Room
inventory-room.description:
This room demonstrates the inventory sidebar. The bowl on the pedestal grants you a single coin when examined; in managed mode the wrapper would populate the inventory from Archipelago state instead.

Note: standalone mode does not auto-populate inventory — the inventory sidebar will appear empty unless something explicitly adds to it.

items.label: bowl on a pedestal
items.description: A wooden bowl sits on a stone pedestal. (Inventory grants happen in managed mode; this is a demo placeholder.)

discovery-room.title: Discovery Room
discovery-room.description:
This room demonstrates discovery mode. With `discoveryMode: 'discovered'`, undiscovered items are hidden and exits to undiscovered rooms are obscured. By default the engine runs in `'full'` mode and shows everything.

Three items are present, but if discovery were enabled you'd only see the ones you'd already examined.

exits.label: a faint outline of a door (unknown destination)
